QF.id: VB Legal Structure of Mutual Funds_9

question.text: What is an ARN in the context of mutual fund distribution?

answer.text: AMFI Registration Number — a unique number given to registered mutual fund distributors
Annual Return Number — the yearly return percentage of a fund
Asset Registration Notice — a document for new fund launches
Audit Reference Number — used for tax filing

explanation.text: **The Logic:** ARN stands for AMFI Registration Number. It is a unique identification number assigned to every registered mutual fund distributor by AMFI. To obtain an ARN, you must pass the required NISM certification exam (Series V-A or V-B) and register with AMFI. Without a valid ARN, you cannot sell mutual funds and earn commissions. **The Trap:** The other options are fictional terms. ARN is specific to mutual fund distribution and is your license to operate as a distributor. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under distributor registration. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Your ARN is your professional identity as a mutual fund distributor. Always quote your ARN on all application forms and communications. Keep it active by renewing your NISM certification before it expires.
